records.stdout:
Top-level CLI parser for the g3ts binary

Usage: g3ts <COMMAND>

Commands:
  init      Writes repo or workspace setup
  validate  Reports repo or workspace validation findings
  help      Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -h, --help     Print help
  -V, --version  Print version

G3TS enforces TypeScript repo setup and TypeScript workspace guardrails.

Package manager:
  G3TS requires pnpm-managed TypeScript workspaces.

Adoption order:
  1. From the Git repo root, run: g3ts init repo
  2. Choose every top-level package.json root that G3TS should manage.
  3. For each chosen root, run: g3ts init workspace --path <path>
  4. For each chosen root, run: g3ts validate workspace --path <path>
  5. After workspace adoption, run: g3ts validate repo

Workspace path choices:
  Root-only package: use .
  App with I/O: use apps/<name> for CLIs, APIs, servers, UI apps, and other executable surfaces.
  Library without I/O: use packages/<name> for reusable packages consumed by other software.

Concepts:
  repo       Git repository surface: hooks, repo-level topology, marker pairs.
  workspace  One adopted TypeScript unit: package.json plus guardrail3-ts.toml.

Rules:
  init writes setup.
  validate only reports.
  validate repo checks that Git will run G3TS.
  validate workspace checks one TypeScript unit.
  Each top-level package.json should be managed by G3TS unless it is intentionally outside the TypeScript guardrail surface.

Writes repo or workspace setup

Usage: g3ts init <COMMAND>

Commands:
  repo       Bootstraps repo-level hook guardrails
  workspace  Bootstraps one adopted TypeScript workspace or package root
  help       Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -h, --help  Print help

Bootstraps repo-level hook guardrails

Usage: g3ts init repo [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --path <PATH>  Path inside the repo to initialize. Defaults to the current directory [default: .]
      --force        Allows bounded managed-file rewrites and managed-block insertion
  -h, --help         Print help

Bootstraps one adopted TypeScript workspace or package root

Usage: g3ts init workspace [OPTIONS] --path <PATH>

Options:
      --path <PATH>  Workspace root to initialize
      --force        Allows bounded generated rewrites
  -h, --help         Print help

Reports repo or workspace validation findings

Usage: g3ts validate <COMMAND>

Commands:
  repo       Validates repository-level invariants (hooks, tools, topology, marker pairs)
  workspace  Validates one TypeScript workspace root against the selected families
  help       Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -h, --help  Print help

Validates one TypeScript workspace root against the selected families

Usage: g3ts validate workspace [OPTIONS] --path <PATH>

Options:
      --path <PATH>      Workspace root to validate
      --family <FAMILY>  Optional family filter [possible values: eslint, astro, astro-setup, astro-content, astro-mdx, astro-i18n, astro-media, astro-seo, astro-state, arch, apparch, tsconfig, package, npmrc, jscpd, style, fmt, spelling, typecov, hooks, topology]
      --inventory        Includes inventory findings in the rendered output
      --staged           Filters toolchain gates by staged files
      --rules-only       Skips toolchain gates and runs only the static rule families
  -h, --help             Print help

Validates repository-level invariants (hooks, tools, topology, marker pairs)

Usage: g3ts validate repo [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --path <PATH>  Optional repo root override; defaults to git rev-parse --show-toplevel
      --inventory    Includes inventory findings in the rendered output
  -h, --help         Print help
